data.content:
### Install Microk8s Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Initiates the installation process for Microk8s.

```Bash
microk8s install
```

--------------------------------

### Setting up Local Development Environment - Shell

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Docs/Content/installation/local-development.md

These commands guide the user through cloning the OneUptime repository, copying the example configuration file, and starting the development server using npm. Prerequisites include Docker, Docker Compose, Node.js, and NPM.

```Shell
# Clone this repo and cd into it.
git clone https://github.com/OneUptime/oneuptime.git
cd oneuptime

# Copy config.example.env to config.env
cp config.example.env config.env

# Since this is dev, you don't have to edit any of those values in config.env. You can, but that's optional.
npm run dev
```

--------------------------------

### Install Rootless Docker

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/LLM/Readme.md

Installs the necessary uidmap package and runs the Docker rootless setup tool to configure Docker to run as a non-root user.

```bash
sudo apt-get install -y uidmap
dockerd-rootless-setuptool.sh install
```

--------------------------------

### Verify Docker Installation

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/LLM/Readme.md

Checks the installed Docker version and lists currently running containers to verify that Docker is installed correctly and the daemon is running without errors.

```bash
docker --version
docker ps
```

--------------------------------

### Install Docker on Ubuntu

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/LLM/Readme.md

Updates the package list and downloads and executes the official Docker installation script for Linux distributions like Ubuntu.

```bash
sudo apt-get update
sudo curl -sSL https://get.docker.com/ | sh
```

--------------------------------

### Example Install Command (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Devops/docs/readme.md

An example command to run the `install.sh` script with specific arguments. This example shows how to install for a database named 'oneuptime' without specifying a username or password, overriding the defaults.

```bash
bash install.sh -u '' -p '' -n 'oneuptime'
```

--------------------------------

### Install OneUptime with Git and npm - Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Docs/Content/installation/docker-compose.md

Clones the OneUptime repository from GitHub, changes into the directory, switches to the 'release' branch, copies the example configuration file, and starts the application using the npm start script. Requires Git and npm installed.

```Bash
# Clone this repo and cd into it.
git clone https://github.com/OneUptime/oneuptime.git
cd oneuptime

# Please make sure you're on release branch.
git checkout release

# Copy config.example.env to config.env
cp config.example.env config.env

# IMPORTANT: Edit config.env file. Please make sure you have random secrets.

npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Install Git LFS

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/LLM/Readme.md

Installs Git Large File Storage, which is required for cloning large model files from repositories like Hugging Face.

```bash
git lfs install
```

--------------------------------

### Starting the Development Server (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Accounts/README.md

Runs the application in development mode. Opens the app in a browser at http://localhost:3000. The page will automatically reload upon edits, and lint errors will be displayed in the console.

```Shell
npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Get Kubernetes Storage Classes Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Lists the available Kubernetes storage classes configured in the cluster, used to verify the hostpath storage class is available after enabling the addon.

```Bash
kubectl get storageclass
```

--------------------------------

### Building for Production with Create React App (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Dashboard/README.md

Builds the application for production deployment, outputting optimized and minified files into the `build` folder. This prepares the app for best performance in a production environment.

```Shell
npm run build
```

--------------------------------

### Starting Development Server with Create React App (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Dashboard/README.md

Runs the application in development mode, typically opening it in a browser at http://localhost:3000. The server automatically reloads on code changes and shows lint errors in the console.

```Shell
npm start
```

--------------------------------

### Start OneUptime Infrastructure Agent (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/InfrastructureAgent/README.md

Starts the OneUptime Infrastructure Agent service after it has been installed and configured.

```bash
oneuptime-infrastructure-agent start
```

--------------------------------

### View Install Script Help (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Devops/docs/readme.md

Displays the help message for the `install.sh` script, listing available command-line arguments and their functions. This is useful for understanding how to configure the installation process.

```bash
bash install.sh -h
```

--------------------------------

### Get Kubernetes Storage Classes After Ceph Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Lists the available Kubernetes storage classes after enabling the Rook Ceph addon, used to verify that Ceph storage classes are available.

```Bash
kubectl get storageclass
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Example SPF TXT Record

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Haraka/README.md

Provides a more complex example of an SPF TXT record. It includes authorizing MX and A records, a specific IP address (`ip4`), and includes SPF records from other domains (`include`).

```txt
v=spf1 mx a ip4:37.26.74.9 include:_spf.google.com include:mail.genosyn.com ~all
```

--------------------------------

### Run AI Docker Container

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/LLM/Readme.md

Executes the 'start-ai' script defined in the project's package.json file, which is typically used to run the Docker container for the AI component.

```npm
npm run start-ai
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Microk8s DNS Addon Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Enables the DNS addon in Microk8s, providing in-cluster DNS resolution for services and pods.

```Bash
microk8s enable dns
```

--------------------------------

### Start OneUptime Infrastructure Agent for Development (Go/Bash)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/InfrastructureAgent/README.md

Starts the locally built OneUptime Infrastructure Agent for testing and development. Requires sudo privileges.

```bash
sudo ./oneuptime-infrastructure-agent start
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Microk8s Dashboard Addon Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Enables the Kubernetes Dashboard addon in Microk8s, providing a web-based user interface for managing cluster resources.

```Bash
microk8s enable dashboard
```

--------------------------------

### Build AI Docker Image

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/LLM/Readme.md

Executes the 'build-ai' script defined in the project's package.json file, which is typically used to build the Docker image for the AI component.

```npm
npm run build-ai
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Microk8s Hostpath Storage Addon Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Enables the hostpath storage provisioner addon in Microk8s, allowing persistent volumes to be stored directly on the host machine's filesystem.

```Bash
microk8s enable hostpath-storage
```

--------------------------------

### Complete FluentBit Configuration Example

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Docs/Content/telemetry/fluentbit.md

This is a complete example of a fluent-bit.yaml configuration file. It includes the 'service' section, an example 'http' input, the necessary 'opentelemetry_envelope' and 'content_modifier' processors, and the 'opentelemetry' output configured to send data to OneUptime.

```yaml
service:
  flush: 1
  log_level: info

pipeline:
  inputs:
    - name: http
      listen: 0.0.0.0
      port: 8888

      processors:
        logs:
          - name: opentelemetry_envelope

          - name: content_modifier
            context: otel_resource_attributes
            action: upsert
            key: service.name
            value: YOUR_SERVICE_NAME

  outputs:
    - name: stdout
      match: '*'
    - name: opentelemetry
      match: '*'
      host: 'oneuptime.com'
      port: 443
      metrics_uri: '/otlp/v1/metrics'
      logs_uri: '/otlp/v1/logs'
      traces_uri: '/otlp/v1/traces'
      tls: On
      header:
        - x-oneuptime-token YOUR_TELEMETRY_INGESTION_TOKEN
```

--------------------------------

### Wait for Microk8s Status Ready Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Checks the status of Microk8s and waits until all components are reported as ready.

```Bash
microk8s status --wait-ready
```

--------------------------------

### Clone LLM Model from Hugging Face

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/LLM/Readme.md

Changes directory to the Models folder and clones the specified Meta Llama 3 8B Instruct model repository from Hugging Face using Git LFS.

```bash
cd ./LLM/Models
git clone https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Microk8s Kubeconfig Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Exports the Microk8s kubectl configuration to the standard Kubernetes config file location (~/.kube/config) for easy access.

```Bash
sudo microk8s kubectl config view --raw > ~/.kube/config
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Microk8s Rook Ceph Addon Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Enables the Rook Ceph addon in Microk8s, setting up a distributed storage system using Ceph. This is typically used in multi-node clusters.

```Bash
microk8s enable rook-ceph
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Microk8s MetalLB Addon Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Enables the MetalLB load balancer addon in Microk8s, configuring it with a specific IP address range for allocating external IPs to services.

```Bash
microk8s enable metallb:192.168.0.105-192.168.0.111
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Example DMARC TXT Record

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Haraka/README.md

Presents a concrete example of a DMARC TXT record value for the `_dmarc` subdomain. This example sets a strict reject policy (`p=reject`) and directs aggregate and forensic reports to specified email addresses for the `genosyn.com` domain.

```txt
_dmarc  TXT "v=DMARC1; p=reject; adkim=s; aspf=r; rua=mailto:dmarc-feedback@genosyn.com; ruf=mailto:dmarc-feedback@genosyn.com; pct=100"
```

--------------------------------

### Ejecting from Create React App (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Dashboard/README.md

Removes the single build dependency and copies all configuration files (webpack, Babel, ESLint, etc.) into the project. This provides full control over the build setup but is a one-way operation.

```Shell
npm run eject
```

--------------------------------

### Install OneUptime Infrastructure Agent (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/InfrastructureAgent/README.md

Downloads and executes the official installation script for the OneUptime Infrastructure Agent using curl and bash.

```bash
curl -s https://oneuptime.com/docs/static/scripts/infrastructure-agent/install.sh | bash
```

--------------------------------

### Test GPU Access with Docker

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/LLM/Readme.md

Runs a sample CUDA application provided by NVIDIA within a Docker container, configured to use all available GPUs, to verify that Docker can access and utilize the system's GPUs.

```bash
docker run --rm -it --gpus=all nvcr.io/nvidia/k8s/cuda-sample:nbody nbody -gpu -benchmark
```

--------------------------------

### Build OneUptime Infrastructure Agent (Go/Bash)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/InfrastructureAgent/README.md

Commands to prepare Go modules, install dependencies, and build the OneUptime Infrastructure Agent executable from source code.

```bash
go mod tidy
go install
go build
```

--------------------------------

### Running Tests with Create React App (Shell)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Dashboard/README.md

Launches the test runner in interactive watch mode. This command is used to execute project tests and monitor file changes to re-run tests automatically.

```Shell
npm test
```

--------------------------------

### Uninstall Microk8s Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Removes the Microk8s installation from the system.

```Bash
microk8s uninstall
```

--------------------------------

### Run Installation Script

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Devops/backup/Readme.md

Executes the `install.sh` script using bash, which is expected to set up the daily backup cron job. Notes that `sudo` might be required if the command fails.

```bash
#if this fails use sudo
bash install.sh
```

--------------------------------

### Start Backup Timer (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Devops/docs/readme.md

Uses `systemctl` to start the `backup.timer`. This resumes the automatic execution of the backup service according to its schedule.

```bash
sudo systemctl start backup.timer
```

--------------------------------

### Install Kubectl and Configure Doctl

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Devops/backup/Readme.md

Installs the `kubectl` command-line tool for interacting with Kubernetes clusters and configures `doctl` (DigitalOcean CLI) to save the cluster's kubeconfig.

```bash
#Install Kubectl
OS_ARCHITECTURE="amd64"
if [[ "$(uname -m)" -eq "aarch64" ]] ; then OS_ARCHITECTURE="arm64" ; fi
if [[ "$(uname -m)" -eq "arm64" ]] ; then OS_ARCHITECTURE="arm64" ; fi
echo "RUNNING COMMAND: curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/$(OS_ARCHITECTURE)/kubectl"
curl -LO https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/$(curl -s https://storage.googleapis.com/kubernetes-release/release/stable.txt)/bin/linux/$(OS_ARCHITECTURE)/kubectl
echo "RUNNING COMMAND: chmod +x ./kubectl"
chmod +x ./kubectl
echo "RUNNING COMMAND: sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl"
sudo mv ./kubectl /usr/local/bin/kubectl

# Get kube config. This is specific to digital ocean.
doctl kubernetes cluster kubeconfig save <cluster-id>
```

--------------------------------

### Run .NET OpenTelemetry Example Locally

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Examples/otel-dotnet/README.md

This command sets environment variables for OpenTelemetry OTLP exporter headers (including a OneUptime token) and endpoint (localhost), then runs the .NET application listening on port 7856. This configuration directs telemetry data to a local endpoint.

```bash
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS="x-oneuptime-token=51d01130-cf14-11ee-a74d-1364f8ef0ac6" && export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT="http://localhost" && dotnet run --urls=http://localhost:7856/
```

--------------------------------

### Start OneUptime with Docker Compose Directly - Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Docs/Content/installation/docker-compose.md

Provides an alternative method to start OneUptime using Docker Compose directly, without relying on npm. It reads environment variables from `config.env` and runs `docker compose up` in detached mode, removing orphaned containers. Includes a version using `sudo` for potential permission issues. Requires Docker and Docker Compose installed.

```Bash
# Read env vars from config.env file and run docker compose up.
(export $(grep -v '^#' config.env | xargs) && docker compose up --remove-orphans -d)

# Use sudo if you're having permission issues with binding ports.
sudo bash -c "(export $(grep -v '^#' config.env | xargs) && docker compose up --remove-orphans -d)"
```

--------------------------------

### Add Microk8s Aliases to Bashrc Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Adds aliases to the bash profile (~/.bashrc) to use `kubectl` and `helm` commands directly, automatically prefixing them with `microk8s`.

```Bash
alias kubectl='microk8s kubectl'\nalias helm='microk8s helm3'
```

--------------------------------

### Example Restore Command (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Devops/docs/readme.md

An example command to run the `restore.sh` script with specific arguments. This command restores a specified backup file ('oneuptime-backup-1613551425.archive') to a database named 'oneuptime' without using a username or password.

```bash
/root/oneuptime_bk_files bash restore.sh -f oneuptime-backup-1613551425.archive -p '' -u ''
```

--------------------------------

### Run .NET OpenTelemetry Example on Test Server

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Examples/otel-dotnet/README.md

This command sets environment variables for OpenTelemetry OTLP exporter headers (with a different OneUptime token) and endpoint (test-otlp.oneuptime.com), then runs the .NET application listening on port 7856. This configuration directs telemetry data to the OneUptime test server.

```bash
export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS="x-oneuptime-token=3be58190-c7ec-11ee-8e5e-3952f961cde5" && export OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT="https://test-otlp.oneuptime.com" && dotnet run --urls=http://localhost:7856/
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Haraka Docker Stack

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Haraka/README.md

Navigates to the `haraka` directory and deploys a Docker stack named `haraka` using the `docker-compose.yml` file. This command is used to start or update the services defined in the compose file.

```bash
cd haraka
sudo docker stack deploy haraka -c docker-compose.yml
```

--------------------------------

### Edit Bashrc File Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Opens the user's bash profile file (~/.bashrc) in the `vi` editor to add custom configurations or aliases.

```Bash
vi ~/.bashrc
```

--------------------------------

### Start OneUptime Probe with Docker Compose

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/Docs/Content/probe/custom-probe.md

Command to start the OneUptime probe service defined in the `docker-compose.yml` file in detached mode.

```Bash
docker compose up -d
```

--------------------------------

### Install OneUptime Helm Chart (Console)

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Public/oneuptime/README.md

Add the official OneUptime Helm repository and then install the chart into your Kubernetes cluster, using the configurations defined in your values.yaml file.

```console
helm repo add oneuptime https://helm-chart.oneuptime.com/
helm install my-oneuptime oneuptime/oneuptime -f values.yaml
```

--------------------------------

### Purge Multipass Instances Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Purges all deleted Multipass instances, freeing up disk space. Used in conjunction with `multipass delete`.

```Bash
multipass purge
```

--------------------------------

### Connect External Ceph to Microk8s Bash

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/HelmChart/Docs/MicroK8s.md

Connects Microk8s to an external Ceph cluster, allowing it to use the external Ceph storage. This is used when Rook Ceph is enabled.

```Bash
sudo microk8s connect-external-ceph
```

--------------------------------

### API Request Body - JSON

Source: https://github.com/oneuptime/oneuptime/blob/master/APIReference/CodeExamples/Model/CountRequest.md

Example JSON body for an API request, showing the optional 'query' object.

```JSON
{
    "query": {
        // query object (optional, if left optional it'll select everything)
    }
}
```
